# LinkedIn Post — OpenAI Closes $122B Funding at $852B Valuation, Kills Sora, Pivots to Enterprise
# Week: Week of 2026-03-30
# Brand Voice: GYanTech AI — Visionary, executive audience, lead generation
# Status: Review before posting
# Auto-generated: 2026-04-05T23:02:30.015Z

---

The largest private funding round in history just redefined what an AI company is willing to sacrifice for focus.

OpenAI closed $122 billion at an $852 billion valuation — then immediately killed Sora, its video generation product, after it bled $1 million per day against a user base that collapsed from 1 million to under 500,000. The company is now channeling resources into coding tools, enterprise products, and an integrated "AI Super App" combining ChatGPT, Codex, browsing, and agent functions.

For executives, this is not just a product strategy story. It is a capital allocation signal. The most well-funded AI company on Earth just decided that generalist experimentation is over. The next phase is vertical depth — enterprise workflows, developer tooling, and agent-driven automation that compounds inside organizations.

The implication is stark: if OpenAI is narrowing its aperture to win the enterprise, every company currently building AI strategy around broad consumer-facing tools should recalibrate. The platforms your teams integrate today will look fundamentally different in 12 months. The winners will be organizations that architect for modularity now — so they can absorb these shifts without ripping out foundations.

GYanTech AI helps leadership teams build AI strategies designed for exactly this kind of platform volatility -> www.gyantechai.com

If OpenAI is willing to kill a flagship product at $1M/day burn to sharpen enterprise focus, what does that signal about where your AI vendor's priorities actually are?

---
## Fact-check notes
- OpenAI announced completion of $122 billion in funding with a post-money valuation of $852 billion on March 31, 2026 (Source: Amiko Consulting weekly roundup, citing OpenAI announcement)
- OpenAI terminated Sora after users declined from ~1 million at peak to under 500,000, with the product consuming approximately $1 million per day in costs (Source: Reuters via Amiko Consulting)
- OpenAI is reallocating resources toward coding and enterprise tools in response to competition from Google and Anthropic (Source: Reuters via Amiko Consulting)
